channel.title: dl.acm.org on Chege's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in dl.acm.org on Chege's Blog

item.title: Myths and Fallacies of 'Personally Identifiable Information'
item.description:
Myths and Fallacies of 'Personally Identifiable Information'. Narayanan, Arvind; Shmatikov, Vitaly. dl.acm.org . Jun 1, 2010.

What is PII?

From Breach Notification Laws:

For example, California Senate Bill 1386: SSNs, driver’s license numbers, financial accounts.

The list can never be exhaustive, e.g. email addresses and telephone numbers are not mentioned in Bill 1386.

Focuses on data that are commonly used for authenticating an individual. Ignores data that reveals some sensitive information about an individual

item.title: Calculators
item.description:
Android Calculator

Making a precise calculator is not trivial. While \(10^{100} + 1 - 10^{100} = 1\), iOS’s calculator gives back \(0\). explores how Android’s calculator gets this right courtesy of .

Real number representations are fundamentally imprecise because we can’t squeeze in infinitely many real numbers into a finite number of bits. Floating-point representations have a base \(\beta\) (assumed to be even) and a precision \(p\), e.g., if \(\beta = 10\) and \(p = 3\), then \(0.1\) is represented as \(1.00 \times 10^{-1}\). IEEE 754, a dominant floating point representation, requires \(\beta = 2, p = 24\) for single precision and \(p = 53\) for double precision. In IEEE 754, \(0.1\) cannot be represented exactly as \(1/10\) is not a factor of \(2\); it is approximately \(1.10011001100110011001101 \times 2^{-4}\).

item.title: Testing in a Monorepo
item.description:
Testing Web Components

While any test framework can work, it’s better to test web components in a browser environment because that’s where they’ll be used. Node-based frameworks would require too much shimming of DOM calls that’d make the tests unrepresentative. and are good options for browser-based testing.

is powered by ES-build, and so is the client-side of the app; let’s go down this path and see where it leads. CommonJS modules don’t work in the browser . Earlier on , using ESM on the server turned out difficult. Hopefully, that doesn’t me bite back now.

item.title: ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media (HT)
item.description:
HT ‘21: Proceedings of the 32nd ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media

computed an h-index of 4 for HT ‘19. So HT ‘21 is unlikely to be influential as far as web research goes. That may explain why WWW is being brought into the ACM’s SIGWEB .

  1. Proceedings of the 32nd ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media. dl.acm.org . www.sigweb.org . Aug 30, 2021.
  2. HT 2019 - Proceedings of the 30th ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media. www.scimagojr.com . Accessed Oct 12, 2021.
  3. The Web Conference (to join ACM SIGWeb in 2022). www.sigweb.org . Accessed Oct 12, 2021.

item.title: The Web Conference
item.description:
The Web Conference is slated to be a part of ACM’s SIGWEB from 2022 . The papers in WWW also cover topics that are not directly related to the web, e.g. recommendations, machine learning, etc.

ACM’s Special Interest Groups seem pretty important, so they should be a good source of current ideas. The annual Hypertext and Social Media Conference (HT) should be a good supplement to WWW. Browsing HT ‘21 reveals that it has more web-related topics, but the number of submissions is much lower than that of WWW ‘21.

item.title: Experiments/Theories in Socioeconomics
item.description:
Programmable Money

South Korea’s COVID-19 stimulus payments were programmed to expire in Aug 2020, and could only be spent at qualifying shops. Australia’s cashless welfare card [currently in pilot stages] cannot be withdrawn as cash, nor used at alcohol, tobacco and gambling joints.

Monzo, a UK-based digital-first mobile baking app, supports IFTTT recipes. This enables budgeting logic, “If I spend at KFC, then move $5 to my penalty pot”.

The Self-Esteem Craze in America

In the 80s and 90s, there was research that low self-esteem was related to undesirable results, but there wasn’t any conclusive work that high esteem causes good outcomes. The self-esteem craze was defined by a simple inspiring message, a lot of half-baked research, prevalent confirmation bias, cottage-industry (books, videos, seminars). Self-esteem grew into a panacea for academic performance, crime, teen pregnancy until more rigorous research arrived. Similar overhyped[?] panaceas: power posing, growth mindset, grit.
